title: Regression: podman network: systemd .network can't use IP address in host subnet while .json config can
body:
### Issue Description

podman bridge network with host-local network_interface for existing bridge and same subnet can exist if it's configured via .json but not .network systemd unit file

Tested with version 6.0.0-dev:
- An existing interface bridge0 `inet 172.16.50.2/24 brd 172.16.50.255 scope global bridge0`

Consider the following `/etc/containers/networks/br0.json`
```json
{
     "name": "br0",
     "id": "9489d7cfe99debfb567f08fc948dd5f1b5799b45eb60a72d837b599797ae71d6",
     "driver": "bridge",
     "network_interface": "bridge0",
     "created": "2025-10-18T10:34:38.804821733Z",
     "subnets": [
          {
               "subnet": "172.16.50.0/24",
               "gateway": "172.16.50.1"
          }
     ],
     "ipv6_enabled": true,
     "internal": false,
     "dns_enabled": false,
     "ipam_options": {
          "driver": "host-local"
     }
}
```

And the following `/etc/containers/systemd/br0.network`:

```
[Unit]
Description=Podman root host network

[Network]
Gateway=172.16.50.1
Driver=bridge
InterfaceName=bridge0
IPAMDriver=host-local
Subnet=172.16.50.0/24
IPv6=true
```

With `br0.json`, the network will both be created and containers can use it with a static IP from that subnet, if they're privileged containers (NOTE: replace `Network` option with `br0.network` if testing with podman systemd .network file)

Example `/etc/containers/systemd/adguardhome.container`:
```
[Unit]
Description=AdGuardHome Quadlet
After=local-fs.target

[Container]
AutoUpdate=registry
Environment="TZ=Europe/Paris"
Image=docker.io/adguard/adguardhome:latest
Volume=adguardhome:/opt/adguardhome:z
IP=172.16.50.7
Network=br0
Unmask=all
SecurityLabelDisable=true
AddCapability=all
SeccompProfile=unconfined


[Install]
# Start by default on boot
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

[Service]
Restart=always
TimeoutStartSec=60
```


With br0.network, the following error occurs: 
```
Oct 24 14:32:22 server br0-network[7091]: Error: subnet 172.16.50.0/24 is already used on the host or by another config
Oct 24 14:32:22 server systemd[1]: br0-network.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Use podman compiled from source following steps from https://podman.io/docs/installation 
2. Copy aforementioned `/etc/containers/networks/br0.json` in the respective location, adjust network settings as needed
3. Copy aforementioned `/etc/containers/systemd/adguardhome.container` (or any privileged container with static IP; adjust IP as needed) in the respective location
4. Start network and container
5. Note that the container is accessible at the set IP-address.
6. Check and copy result of `sudo podman network inspect br0`
7. Stop container and delete/disable network
8. Copy aforementioned `/etc/containers/systemd/br0.network` in the respective location
9. Modify `/etc/containers/systemd/adguardhome.container` to use `Network=br0.network`
11. `sudo systemctl daemon-reload`
12. `sudo systemctl start adguardhome` - or just the network
13. Check `sudo journalctl -xeu br0-network.service` - should closely match aforementioned error message
14. Remove/comment out settings `Gateway` and `Subnet` in `/etc/containers/systemd/br0.network`
15. `sudo systemctl daemon-reload`
16. `sudo systemctl start br0-network` - network now starts successfully and should show up in `sudo podman network ls`
17. Inspect network with `sudo podman network inspect systemd-br0`
18. Note that the configuration is (apart from UUID, name and the IP settings which had to be changed to have the network running for inspection) identical to the one created with `br0.json`

### Describe the results you received

Network with same settings can be created with a .json configuration but not with a .network systemd unit.

Systemd unit causes error:
```
Oct 24 14:32:22 server br0-network[7091]: Error: subnet 172.16.50.0/24 is already used on the host or by another config
Oct 24 14:32:22 server systemd[1]: br0-network.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
```


### Describe the results you expected

Identical configuration should create the same result. Personally, I hope that the behavior of br0.json is the expected behavior, as assigning an IP of a container to a host bridge subnet should be possible.

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Ubuntu Server 25.10 using NetworkManager. No snaps.

### Additional information

N/A

comments.body:
> subnet 172.16.50.0/24 is already used on the host or by another config

This is just a normal error on podman network create so this seems unrelated to using quadlet files.
You are trying to setup a normal bridge network with a subnet that already exists on the host so this errors out because the normal "managed" bridge assume we fully own the interface as we create our own NAT rules etc...

Creating the json file by hand bypasses such checks but likely does not result in the outcome you want as you then get the netavark creating masquerading rules.
It sounds like you are looking for the unmanaged mode `--opt mode=unmanaged`/`Options=mode=unmanaged` instead which is designed to work on existing bridge interfaces.

Ah, thanks for that information. I'll try out unmanaged mode then, and creating the host brige via podman without an existing interface.

In that case the ticket can be closed.
I'll assume @Luap99 nailed this one down and will close.
